findings.description: Cross-call prompt injection (PREVIOUSLY REPORTED): _build_hypothesis_summary() at lines 203-266 extracts LLM-generated text fields (core_logic, bear_case, hypothesis, layer_N_narrative) from HypothesisResult objects and embeds them into the decision prompt via f-strings. This means investigation LLM output flows unsanitized into the decision LLM input. MITIGATION: chat_pro() at gateway/async_client.py applies input_guard sanitization to both system and user prompts (confirmed in integration audit). However, input_guard is a generic sanitizer — it does not understand the semantic structure of the decision prompt and could miss context-specific injection patterns.

Signal conflict text injection (NEW): _detect_signal_conflicts() at lines 269-314 generates Chinese-language conflict descriptions that contain hypothesis text and computed divergence values. These are embedded into the decision prompt at lines 175-183 via f-strings. The conflict descriptions include: hypothesis text from refined_hypothesis field, divergence calculations, and Chinese natural language that could contain LLM-generated content from upstream modules (causal_decomposition, flow_decomposition, scenario_forecaster, fragility_scanner). Same cross-call pattern as finding #1.

Existing prompt format integrity (VERIFIED): Signal conflict summary is ADDITIVE — inserted between hypothesis summary and Signal Resonance section (line 185). The base prompt structure (Resonance → L1 → L2 → L3 → Red Team → instructions) remains intact. The conflict section is prefixed with a Chinese header '信号冲突 (ANALYST_DISAGREEMENT)' and includes an instruction to address conflicts. This does NOT break or corrupt the existing prompt format.

Backward compatibility — VERIFIED: hypotheses parameter defaults to None (line 115). _build_decision_prompt receives it (line 128). _build_hypothesis_summary handles None with 'if hypotheses else ""' (line 213, 263-264). Empty list handled — returns "" (line 263-264). No crash, no TypeError. Existing callers without hypotheses parameter continue to work unchanged.

Position size enforcement: DECISION_SYSTEM_PROMPT specifies 25% total heat limit (line 97-98) but enforcement is prompt-only — _parse_decision_response() does not programmatically cap position_size_pct values. An LLM output exceeding 25% would pass through unchecked.

notes: The HIGH cross-call prompt injection finding from the previous audit (2026-05-18T03:34:06Z) persists — LLM-generated text flows from investigation/causal/flow/scenario/fragility modules into the decision prompt without sanitization at the decision.py level. MITIGATION: gateway/async_client.py chat_pro() applies input_guard to both prompts. However, this is defense-in-depth (gateway-level) rather than defense-at-source (decision.py). The NEW signal conflict text injection follows the same cross-call pattern. Recommendation: add sanitize_for_llm_prompt() calls in _build_hypothesis_summary() and the conflict summary section, even though gateway-level input_guard provides a safety net. The signal conflict feature itself is well-designed — additive section that does not break existing prompt structure.
